title: Water Quality: A Foundation for Health and Development
lede: Access to safe drinking water is essential for health and development, yet over 2 billion people, mostly in low- and middle-income countries, still lack it. Many remain exposed to contaminated water, which threatens health, livelihoods, and economic progress.
caption: Access to water from improved sources that is free from contamination (% of population), 2015–24

facts.text: people lacked access to safely managed drinking water in 2024.

title: Powering Progress: The Uneven Path to Electricity Access
lede: Although global electricity access has expanded steadily over the past two decades, millions remain without power—especially in Sub-Saharan Africa—and progress is deeply uneven.
caption: Access to electricity (% of population), ~2015–23

facts.text: key fact from the electricity access story (see full chapter).

title: Past, Present, and Future: 100 Years of Challenges in Tackling Extreme Poverty
lede: After decades of historic poverty reduction, progress has slowed. Understanding who is left behind is essential for the next phase of the fight against extreme poverty.
caption: Extreme poverty rate (% of population), progress evaluation window

facts.text: key fact from the extreme poverty story.
poverty reduction is at its weakest pace in decades.
poverty is increasingly concentrated in fragile settings.

title: Connecting the World: The Race for Internet Access
lede: Internet access has grown at remarkable speed since the early 2000s, yet large gaps remain between and within countries.
caption: Individuals using the Internet (% of population), progress window

facts.text: key fact from the internet access story.
mobile broadband far surpasses fixed broadband globally.

title: Learning and Work: Building Skills for Prosperity
lede: Each year of schooling improves earnings—but learning outcomes and years of schooling still vary widely across the world.

title: Closing the Prosperity Gap
lede: The Prosperity Gap tracks how far economies are from a prosperity standard—and how that gap is distributed across the world.

lede: While high-income nations rapidly integrate AI, structural barriers—skills, connectivity, language data—shape who can benefit.

title: Gender and Jobs: Closing Persistent Gaps
lede: Women face persistent barriers to work and are more often in vulnerable employment—despite progress in education and services.

title: Urban Development: Cities as Engines of Growth
lede: Cities drive global economic growth, but urbanization is uneven and service delivery lags in many fast-growing urban areas.

title: Climate Risk: Exposure Meets Vulnerability
lede: Climate change intensifies extreme weather. Exposure is widespread, but risks concentrate where vulnerability is high.

title: The State of Development Data
lede: Without reliable data, poverty and learning gaps stay hidden. Some economies still build strong statistical systems with limited resources.
